title: Protocol for Proteome Extraction from Norway Spruce Bark Samples
author: Marcelo Ramires

steps.title: Start with frozen bark samples
steps.instruction: Start with frozen bark samples.

steps.title: Use an IKA mill to grind...
steps.instruction: Use an IKA mill to grind the frozen bark samples into fine dust. It's important to keep the samples always frozen during the grinding process, although it is not necessary to use this specific mill.

steps.title: Weigh 100 mg of the sample...
steps.instruction: Weigh 100 mg of the sample and transfer it into a 2 ml microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Add 10 mg of PVPP to inhibit phenolic compounds
steps.instruction: Add 10 mg of PVPP to inhibit phenolic compounds.

steps.title: Mix the sample with TRIzol™ reagent...
steps.instruction: Mix the sample with TRIzol™ reagent and DTT, then incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysate at 12,000 ×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysate at 12,000 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the clear supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the clear supernatant to a new tube.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.2 mL of chloroform per 1 mL of TRIzol™ reagent used. Shake vigorously to mix.

steps.title: Allow the mixture to incubate for...
steps.instruction: Allow the mixture to incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the organic phase, which...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the organic phase, which contains the proteins, to a new tube. Avoid contamination from the aqueous phase.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.3 mL of 100% ethanol per 1 mL of TRIzol™ reagent used.

steps.title: Mix and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Mix and incubate for 5 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 5 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant to a new tube.

steps.title: Add 1
steps.instruction: Add 1.5 mL of isopropanol per 1 mL of TRIzol™ reagent to the supernatant.

steps.title: Incubate with end-over-end rotation overnight at 4°C
steps.instruction: Incubate with end-over-end rotation overnight at 4°C.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 2 mL of wash solution
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 2 mL of wash solution. Incubate for 20 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat the washing steps twice for...
steps.instruction: Repeat the washing steps twice for a thorough cleaning of the pellet.

steps.title: Add 2 mL of 100% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 2 mL of 100% ethanol to the pellet and vortex to mix. Incubate for 20 minutes at room temperature.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully remove and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Allow the pellet to air dry for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Allow the pellet to air dry for 5 minutes. Avoid over-drying to the point of cracking.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 50 μL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 50 μL of Urea buffer containing 50 mM DTT and proteinase inhibitors.

steps.title: Allow the proteins to resuspend at...
steps.instruction: Allow the proteins to resuspend at room temperature for one hour. Then, incubate with end-over-end rotation overnight at 4°C.
